_meta.description: REAL capture, converted to DetectionEvent shapes. Do not hand-edit the events without updating this file's source pointers below.

sourceRecords.note: First 'initial' read: preroll placement only (AD_PLACEMENT_KIND_START), 1 item.

Second 'initial' read 1.5s later: 18 midroll + 1 postroll placement, preroll already gone from this read — exercises classify()'s union-preroll/latest-midroll-count rule with real data, same pattern as __fmDj0ZJ1Q's reload.

omittedFromExport: A getPlayerResponse confirmation read at 2026-07-02T20:38:05.885Z (count 19, identical adPlacements to the 20:38:01.639Z 'initial' read kept above) — redundant duplicate of an already-kept placement-bearing read.

Fetch-path noise immediately following EACH 'initial' read above (pageVideoId=iYlODtkyw_I but a different/absent videoId): three records per read with videoId=null and note='no videoDetails' (malformed/prefetch reads with no videoDetails at all), one with videoId='ux' (2-character garbage from a non-canonical sibling endpoint — the /youtubei/v1/player/ad_break-class issue classifier.ts's summarizePlacementCounts doc comment describes), and one with an 11-character videoId that is an ad creative's OWN player response through the identical /youtubei/v1/player endpoint (chANMd0ZAuU at 20:38:00.312Z, 4Kz4rkOV8CU at 20:38:01.707Z — the latter is also the lastSourceAVideoId recorded on the DOM badge sightings around that time). All real but exactly what the real pipeline drops: classify() and background.ts's handleIncomingEvent both filter on videoId !== pageVideoId (or a missing videoId) before an event would ever reach a session — omitted here for the identical reason, not fabricated absence.
Paired ad-interrupting-start events at the same three timestamps as the ad-showing-start events kept above (millisecond-identical to them, spike-verified pairing) — redundant.

ad-showing-end/ad-interrupting-end pairs: an UNPAIRED one at 2026-07-02T20:38:33.682Z (content time 0, no matching *-start event — the preroll was already playing when the observer attached, the known DOM gap DomAdEvent's doc comment describes), plus one PAIRED end per midroll (~775.35s/~857.40s/~1198.57s content time, each ~20-25s after its own ad-showing-start above). These end timestamps are NOT millisecond-identical to the ad-showing-start timestamps (they are the ad breaks' END, tens of seconds later) — real but add no classification-relevant information here: since source A placements are present, classify() takes the placementsPresent branch and never calls typeDomEventsByContentTime (the only place DOM event kind/content-time matters); DOM evidence only needs to exist at all (domAdSeen = domAdEvents.length > 0) to add 'DOM' to sources, which the three ad-showing-start events already do.
168 ad-badge-element-seen badge sightings — real but redundant with the ad-showing-start events kept above, same domAdSeen rationale.

The export's first two player_response records (2026-07-02T20:34:11.490Z / 20:34:15.656Z, loggedIn=true, adPlacements empty, plus their own analogous fetch-path noise block) — those belong to the SAME video's rewatched session, extracted separately as rewatch-stripped-iYlODtkyw_I.json.
conversionNotes: adPlacements[].offsetStartMs/offsetEndMs were captured as STRINGS in the raw spike export (e.g. "0", "-1", "223666" — YouTube's protobuf-JSON int64-as-string encoding). Converted to numbers here to match AdPlacementItem's `number | null` field type; no value was changed.

No BEACON events: the Phase 0 spike tool captured only 'player_response' and 'dom_ad_event' records (verified: no 'beacon' record type exists in any spike/exports/*.json). Source C is therefore absent from every fixture in this directory, by construction of the real data, not by omission.
dom_ad_event's raw 'event' field is copied verbatim into DomAdEvent.kind ('ad-showing-start' is a literal value in both the spike tool's schema and the real extension's DomAdEventKind).
_meta.expectedClassification: ADS_SERVED, preroll=true (from the first read, unioned), midrolls=18 (latest placement-bearing read), postroll=true, sources=[PLAYER_RESPONSE, DOM] — matches RESULTS.md §2 row 1's '1/18/1'.
